questions.question: What is Node.js?
options: A JavaScript runtime built on Chrome's V8 engine

questions.question: Which engine powers the Node.js runtime?
Node.js is primarily designed for:
What command is used to initialize a new Node.js project?
Which file contains the metadata and dependency list for a Node project?
How do you import a built-in module in Node.js (CommonJS syntax)?
Which module is used to handle file operations in Node.js?
What does npm stand for?
How do you run a Node.js file named 'app.js'?
What is the global object in Node.js similar to 'window' in browsers?
Which module allows creating a web server in Node.js?
What does a package-lock.json file do?
options: Records exact versions of installed packages

questions.question: In require('./myModule'), what does the './' indicate?
options: It is an external npm package
It is a local file relative to the current directory

questions.question: Which property of 'process' object gives access to command-line arguments?
Node.js is asynchronous and:
What is the purpose of 'module.exports'?
options: To expose functions/objects for use in other files

questions.question: Which character is used to separate version numbers in package.json (e.g., ^1.2.3)?
Which built-in module provides information about the operating system?
What suffix is usually used for Node.js environment variables file?
What does 'I/O' stand for?
Which of these best describes the Node.js event loop?
options: It allows Node.js to perform non-blocking I/O operations despite being single-threaded
It is a loop that restarts the server
It is used for CSS animations

questions.question: What is a 'Buffer' in Node.js?
options: A temporary memory storage for binary data
A tool to slow down requests
A way to cache HTML pages

questions.question: What happens if you use 'fs.readFileSync' instead of 'fs.readFile'?
options: It blocks execution until the file is read
It reads only half the file
It is faster for large files

questions.question: What are Streams in Node.js?
options: Objects that let you read data from a source or write data to a destination in a continuous fashion
A way to connect to a satellite

questions.question: Which method is used to write data to a file, overwriting its current content?
What are the two arguments passed to the http.createServer callback?
Why should you use environment variables (.env)?
options: To make the code look professional
To store sensitive information like API keys securely outside the source code

questions.question: What is 'Callback Hell'?
options: Deeply nested callbacks making code hard to read and maintain
A syntax error in a loop

questions.question: Which event is emitted when a readable stream has no more data to read?
What is a 'Readable' stream?
options: A stream that displays text on screen
A stream from which data can be read
A stream used for writing logs
A stream that checks for grammar errors

questions.question: In process.env.PORT || 3000, what is the role of 3000?
options: It is a fallback/default port if environment variable is not set
It is the maximum number of users
It is the version of Node

questions.question: Which built-in module is used to resolve file paths across different operating systems?
What does the 'EventEmitter' class do?
options: Facilitates communication between objects in Node.js by emitting and listening for named events

questions.question: Which 'npm' flag is used to save a package as a development dependency only?
What is the result of 'console.log(__dirname)'?
options: The name of the current file
The absolute path of the directory containing the current file
The root directory of the hard drive

questions.question: How do you handle error in asynchronous 'fs' methods (callback pattern)?
options: The first argument of the callback is reserved for an error object

questions.question: What is 'Piping' in Node.js streams?
options: The process of connecting a readable stream to a writable stream

questions.question: Which statement is true about Node's single-threaded nature?
options: Node can only handle one request at a time
Node uses internal C++ threads via libuv to handle heavy I/O tasks outside the main thread
Node cannot use more than 100MB of RAM

questions.question: What is the purpose of 'require.resolve()'?
options: To get the absolute path to a module file without loading it

questions.question: What is the default behavior of the 'fs.appendFile' method?
options: Creates a new file or adds data to the end of an existing file

questions.question: What is libuv in Node.js?
options: A multi-platform support library with a focus on asynchronous I/O that manages the thread pool and event loop

questions.question: Describe the 'REPL' in Node.js.
options: Read-Eval-Print-Loop: an interactive shell that executes Node.js code

questions.question: How can you run code in another thread in Node.js?
options: Node.js doesn't support threads at all

questions.question: What happens in the 'Poll' phase of the Event Loop?
options: New I/O events are retrieved and their callbacks are executed

questions.question: What is the difference between setImmediate() and process.nextTick()?
options: process.nextTick() runs immediately after the current operation, before the next event loop phase; setImmediate() runs in the Check phase
Both are used for cooling the CPU

questions.question: What are 'Backpressure' issues in Streams?
options: When the data being read from a source is faster than the destination can write it

questions.question: Which of these is used to prevent the Event Loop from exiting while an object is active?
What is the significance of the 'cluster' module?
options: To take advantage of multi-core systems by creating multiple child processes (workers) that share server ports

questions.question: How does Node.js handle heavy CPU-intensive tasks?
options: It performs them on the main thread, blocking everything
By offloading them to worker threads or child processes to avoid blocking the main event loop

questions.question: What is the purpose of 'AbortController' in Node.js?
options: To cancel asynchronous operations like fetch or stream requests
To force a crash for debugging
